visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) A young man with black hair wearing a black leather jacket over a black sweater stands on a vibrant night market street. He holds a small, golden piece of fried tofu near his mouth, smiling confidently at the camera. The background is filled with blurred lights, colorful neon signs, and people walking through the market. (2) The man shouts excitedly, then the scene shifts to him sitting at a low table in a brightly lit indoor/outdoor food stall. He holds wooden chopsticks and looks at a plate of golden-brown fried tofu topped with pickled cabbage. His expression changes to one of nervous laughter. (3) A friend in a black and white plaid shirt sits next to him, tasting a dark soup dish. The friend immediately gags and coughs, covering his mouth with his hand. The main character looks on with a mix of shock and amusement. (4) Back at the table, a man takes a bite of the tofu and looks up with a distorted face of regret, his eyes rolling back. The main character repeatedly bows his head and puts his hands together in a prayer-like gesture of apology, looking distressed. (5) The final shot is a close-up of many pieces of tofu being deep-fried in a large, dark metal vat of bubbling oil.
visual_base.text_narrative_log: (1) [Logo]: POV 小看臭豆腐的韓國歐巴🇰🇷. [Subtitle]: 今天要吃幾盤臭豆腐？ 오늘 취두부 몇판 먹을거야? (2) [Variety]: 100盤! 100판! (3) [Variety]: (還沒吃先瘋掉) 실성ㅋㅋㅋㅋㅋㅋㅋㅋ (4) [Subtitle]: 朋友：我先走囉 도하: 맛있게 드세요. [Variety]: 哈哈哈哈哈 (5) [Subtitle]: 就是麻辣豆腐呀! 마라 두부네! (6) [Subtitle]: 今天要吃幾個臭豆腐？ 오늘 취두부 몇 개 먹을거에요? (7) [Variety]: 100個! (吃了一口) (8) [Variety]: (上天) KIZULㅋㅋㅋㅋㅋㅋ (9) [Subtitle]: 你們要不要多吃幾個？ 좀 더 드세요! (10) [Variety]: 抱意思 죄송함다... x10000 (11) [Logo]: 臭豆腐：(我也抱意思😂)

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main creator in black leather, 3 male friends/co-stars appearing in different shots.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Asian male with black hair and a youthful face, wearing a black leather jacket over a black high-neck sweater.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A bustling Taiwanese night market with glowing red and yellow signs, street food stalls, and passers-by. Later, the interior of a casual roadside restaurant with wooden walls and industrial fans.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) High-energy male vocal in Korean and Mandarin asking a question about eating tofu. A bouncy, high-tempo comedic BGM (approx. 128 BPM) plays. Background noise of a busy night market (crowd chatter) is audible but mixed low. (2) A sharp impact sound effect accompanies a loud male shout. The BGM continues with a playful, repetitive synth melody. A distinct whoosh transition sound occurs as the scene cuts. (3) Sounds of heavy coughing and gagging from a male voice after tasting food. Background laugh track SFX is mixed in for comedic effect. The BGM ducks slightly to emphasize the vocal distress. (4) Multiple voices continue in a mix of Korean and Mandarin with high vocal arousal and laughter. Sound of chopsticks clattering against a plate. Another whoosh SFX marks a quick visual transition. (5) A rhythmic repetition of the phrase 'Bao Yi Si' (sorry) is edited with a heavy delay and echo effect, synchronized with the character bowing. The BGM reaches a final cadence. A subtle bubbling sound of deep-frying oil is heard briefly at the very end.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video aims to entertain through a comedic cultural exchange challenge. It contrasts the initial overconfidence of Korean men (claiming they can eat 100 pans of stinky tofu) with their visceral, humorous struggle and eventual 'apology' to the dish after experiencing its pungent reality.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The video focuses on the general experience of eating stinky tofu at a night market without highlighting a specific brand.

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video uses typical social media editing tropes (loud SFX, meme-style text, fast cuts) to maximize the comedic impact of the 'culture shock' reaction.
